name: CERT C/C++ Secure Coding
summary: The SEI CERT secure-coding risk model — Severity × Likelihood × Remediation → Priority → Level L1/L2/L3 — and its prioritized-remediation process, as computable policies. Covers CERT C and CERT C++.
description: Maps the SEI CERT Coding Standards' distinctive risk-assessment and prioritized-remediation process onto ponens policies. The individual rules are enforced by static analysis; this pack checks that every finding is risk-assessed (severity/likelihood/remediation), assigned a priority and level, that L1 findings are remediated and never shipped, and that Rules vs Recommendations are handled accordingly. Applies to both CERT C and CERT C++.
source: SEI CERT C and CERT C++ Secure Coding Standards (Carnegie Mellon SEI).
